subject: Geography
title: The Midnight Sun
description: A fast breakdown of The Midnight Sun for builders.

hook.text: At the poles, the sun doesn't set for months in summer—and doesn't rise for months in winter.

buildup.text: Earth's 23.5° tilt means polar regions point toward or away from the sun for entire seasons.

discovery.text: In Svalbard, Norway, the sun stays above the horizon from April to August—no nightfall.

twist.text: In polar winter, the darkness lasts equally long—months of night called the 'polar night.'

climax.text: Polar residents adapt to months without sunrise, using light therapy and strict sleep routines.

punchline.text: At the poles, daylight comes in bulk—all at once or not at all.

quiz.question: What causes the midnight sun at the poles?
options: The poles are closer to the sun
